comments.text: The arxiv version of Kevin Ford's "The distribution of totients" reports at the top of page 3 that "Erdős [8] showed that there are infinitely many totients for which $A(m) > m^{c_4}$ for some positive constant $c_4$. The current record is $c_4 = 0.7039$ [1]." Here, $A$ is the function called $g$ in the problem text above and so the problem is to show $c_4$ can be arbitrarily close to 1. Reference [1] is the following: C. Baker and G. Harman, Shifted primes without large prime factors, Acta Arith. 83 (1998), 331–361. I apologize that I have not verified that that reference contains the stated result. (The site has been updated to address this comment.)
Thanks - in fact this has been improved recently by Lichtman. I'll update now.
